titlestmt.titleproper: Register to the Hans L. Bilger Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Kendall L. Crilly

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8398602.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 00:25

did.unittitle: Hans L. Bilger Papers
did.abstract: Music by the American composer Hans L. Bilger (1896-1968)

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Hans L. Bilger Papers were established in the Music Library of Yale University by Bilger's niece, Linda Murray Berzok, in 2005.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Hans L. Bilger Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 95, The Hans L. Bilger Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The American composer Hans L. Bilger (1896-1968) received the B.M. degree from the Yale School of Music in 1920. He was active in Connecticut in the 1930s. Further biographical information is not available.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Hans L. Bilger Papers consist chiefly of Bilger's compositions, both published and in manuscript. They also include music by other composers, song lyrics, two concert programs, and drawings of historical musical instruments.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Music. II. Miscellaneous.

did.unittitle: Music
Music by Hans L. Bilger
Instrumental Music
[Fugue, organ, C minor] Fugue in C minor : organ / H. L. Bilger.
[Fugue, organ, C minor] Fugue in C minor for organ / H. L. Bilger.
[Fugue, piano, C minor] Fugue for piano in 3 voices / H. L. Bilger.
[Overture, C minor] Overture in C minor / H. L. Bilger.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink, annotations in colored pencil.

did.unittitle: [Overture, C minor] Symphonic overture in C minor / H. L. Bilger.
[Overture, C minor] Overture in C minor / H. L. Bilger.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Ms., in part holograph in ink.

did.unittitle: [Overture, C minor; arr.] Overture - C minor / H. L. Bilger.
[Overture, C minor; arr.] Overture in C minor / H. L. Bilger. – 1920.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arr. for piano, with annotations and additions for organ.

did.unittitle: [Scarecrow dance] Intro - scare-crow dance.
[Sonata, violin, piano, G minor] Sonata in G minor : violin & piano / H. L. Bilger. – September, 1919.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Score only, lacks separate violin part.

did.unittitle: The sun worshippers : Zuni Indian melody.
Sunday morning / music by H. L. Bilger. – 1927.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Identified on typescript list of works as Sunday Morning at Dusseldorf.

did.unittitle: [Variations, piano, G major] Theme with variations for piano / H. L. Bilger.
Wooden shoe dance / H. L. Bilger.
Songs
All in all / words by Bliss Carmen ; music by H. L. Bilger. – Philadelphia : Theodore Presser, c1930.
April / lyric by Theodosia Garrison ; music by H. L. Bilger. – New York : E. Morris Music Pub., c1925.
Clouds / lyric by Sydney Stone Field ; music by Hans L. Bilger. – New York : Edward Morris Music Pub., c1929.
I married a wife / [lyric by] Warren Gilbert ; [music by] H. L. Bilger. – 1928.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in E-flat.

did.unittitle: If I were not a little girl / lyric by Violet Alleyn Storey ; music by H. L. Bilger. – New York : Edward Morris Music Pub., c1927.
Little boy of mine / poem by Alice P. Whiteside ; music by Hans L. Bilger. – New York : Edward Morris Music Pub., c1929.
[Lonely things]

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in F minor.

did.unittitle: Lonely things : song / [lyric by] Helen Magaret ; [music by] H. L. Bilger.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in F minor.
At end: Poem published in Harper's Magazine.

did.unittitle: The Lord of glory / words and music by H. L. Bilger. – Philadelphia : Theodore Presser, c1932.
Mother-mine / [lyric by] Jean Mertinett ; [music by] H. L. Bilger. – c1924.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in B-flat.

did.unittitle: Mother-mine / lyric by Jean Martinett ; music by H. L. Bilger. – New York : Edward Morris Music Pub., c1924.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in D.

did.unittitle: My garden / poem anonymous ; music by H. L. Bilger. – London : Arthur H. Stockwell, c1930.
The path of yesterday / poem by Grantland Rice ; music by H. L. Bilger. – New York : Edward Morris Music Pub., c1928.
A poor unfortunate / lyric by F. L. Stanton ; music by H. L. Bilger.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in E-flat.
At end: Printed in The Atlanta Constitution.

did.unittitle: [Song the grass sings] Encore song : A song the grass sings / lyric by Charles G. Blanden ; music by H. L. Bilger. – c1931.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in F.

did.unittitle: A song the grass sings / poem by Charles G. Blanden ; music by H. L. Bilger. – Boston : Oliver Ditson, c1932.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For voice and piano, in F.

did.unittitle: When I was one and twenty / words by A. E. Housman ; music by H. L. Bilger. – Philadelphia : Theodore Presser, c1927.
Music by Other Composers
[Keyboard music. Selections] Pavana : the Earle of Salisbury [etc.] / William Byrde.
did.physdesc: 2 ms. p. of music ; 27 x 34 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: Pavana : The Earle of Salisbury – The Garman Whistle – Andantino and Coda of Sellengers Round (contains variations)

did.unittitle: Sunny Sue / Walter Donaldson ; arr. by B.

odd.head: General
odd.p: At end: New York City, Dec. 1921.

did.unittitle: Red Godwin's wooing : dramatic ballad for male voices and solo voice / by William E. Haesche.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Possibly holograph, in pencil, ink, and colored ink.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous
Typescript poems for use as lyrics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cherry-Ripe / Anon. Clouds / Sydney Stone. Field Empty Praise / Violet Alleyn Storey. If I Were Not a Little Girl / Violet Alleyn Storey (on verso: Rock Me to Sleep in Georgia Again / Cecil B. Sledge). Little Boy of Mine / Alice P. Whiteside. An Old Fashioned Garden / Author unknown (on verso: Rock Me to Sleep in Georgia Again / Cecil B. Sledge). When I Was One and Twenty / A. E. Housman.

did.unittitle: Programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Virginia College, Roanoke, Virginia, Saturday, December 14, 1929. Bridgeport Federal Symphony Orchestra, Wednesday, May 24 [probably 1939].

did.unittitle: Typescript list of compositions, with annotations (1 p.)
Drawings of historical musical instruments and chant, hand-colored.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Copied from pictures in magazines such as Harpers New Monthly Magazine and The Musician (Boston)
